product_overview: Memory Concierge is a backend orchestration service for session persistence, semantic memory retrieval, deep reasoning, synthesis, and secure response delivery. It supports authenticated memory operations, profile querying, API key and MCP session management, and operational health/metrics endpoints for a second-brain harness.
core_goals: Persist and retrieve session and project memories with vector-backed semantic search.
Support authenticated basic and OAuth-based access flows with token lifecycle management.
Provide subject profiles, intelligence job tracking, and memory analytics for advanced knowledge workflows.
Enable project/API key lifecycle management and MCP proxy-token access controls.
Expose operational endpoints for health, service discovery, and metrics monitoring.

features.name: Health & Readiness
features.description: Expose service liveness, readiness, and overall dependency health for operational monitoring.
user_flows: GET /api/v1/health -> Receive 200 with overall service status and dependency information -> GET /api/v1/health/ready -> Receive 200 when dependencies are reachable -> GET /api/v1/health/live -> Receive 200 confirming the service process is alive

GET /api/v1/health/ready when a dependency is unavailable -> Receive non-200 readiness response indicating the service is not ready -> GET /api/v1/health/live -> Receive 200 confirming the process is still running

features.name: OAuth Authentication
features.description: Support OAuth2 authorization-code and device authorization flows, token exchange, callback handling, client metadata, and token revocation.
user_flows: GET /api/v1/auth/oauth/client-info -> Receive 200 with public OAuth client metadata -> GET /api/v1/auth/oauth/authorize with valid authorization request -> Receive redirect to provider authorization flow -> GET /api/v1/auth/oauth/callback after provider approval -> Receive 200/redirect indicating successful authorization -> POST /api/v1/auth/oauth/token with valid authorization code -> Receive 200 with access token

POST /api/v1/auth/oauth/authorize with invalid or incomplete parameters -> Receive 4xx validation error -> POST /api/v1/auth/oauth/token with an expired or invalid code -> Receive 4xx token exchange failure

POST /api/v1/auth/device with valid device authorization request -> Receive 200 with device/user verification details -> POST /api/v1/auth/revoke with authenticated bearer token -> Receive 200 confirming token revocation
POST /api/v1/auth/revoke without authentication -> Receive 401 unauthorized response

features.name: Basic Authentication
features.description: Allow users to register, log in with email/password, refresh JWT access tokens, and log out.
user_flows: POST /api/v1/auth/basic/register with valid email and password -> Receive 201 indicating account creation -> POST /api/v1/auth/basic/login with valid credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT token and user object -> POST /api/v1/auth/basic/refresh with valid refresh context -> Receive 200 with refreshed access token -> POST /api/v1/auth/basic/logout with bearer token -> Receive 200 confirming session invalidation
POST /api/v1/auth/basic/login with incorrect password -> Receive 401 with invalid credentials error
POST /api/v1/auth/basic/register with invalid email format or weak password -> Receive 4xx validation error
POST /api/v1/auth/basic/logout without bearer token -> Receive 401 unauthorized response

features.name: Memory Management
features.description: Create, list, search, retrieve, update, delete, and bulk-delete tenant-scoped vector memories.
user_flows: POST /api/v1/auth/basic/login with valid credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT token -> POST /api/v1/memories with bearer token and valid title/content/memory_type -> Receive 201 with created memory -> GET /api/v1/memories with bearer token -> Receive 200 with paginated memory list -> POST /api/v1/memories/search with bearer token and query -> Receive 200 with semantically relevant matches -> GET /api/v1/memories/:id with bearer token -> Receive 200 with memory details -> PUT /api/v1/memories/:id with bearer token and updated fields -> Receive 200 with updated memory -> DELETE /api/v1/memories/:id with bearer token -> Receive 200 confirming deletion
POST /api/v1/memories with bearer token and missing required fields -> Receive 400 validation error
POST /api/v1/memories/search with empty query or invalid limit -> Receive 4xx validation error
GET /api/v1/memories/:id for a non-existent memory -> Receive 404 not found
POST /api/v1/memories/bulk/delete with bearer token and a list of ids -> Receive 200 with bulk deletion result
GET /api/v1/memories/admin/stats with bearer token -> Receive 200 with tenant memory statistics

features.name: Intelligence
features.description: Track asynchronous intelligence jobs and retrieve conclusions generated from heavy AI operations.
user_flows: GET /api/v1/intelligence/jobs/:id with bearer token for an existing job -> Receive 200 with job status and progress details -> GET /api/v1/intelligence/conclusions with bearer token -> Receive 200 with available intelligence conclusions -> POST /api/v1/intelligence/flush with bearer token -> Receive 200 confirming queue flush
GET /api/v1/intelligence/jobs/:id with an unknown job id -> Receive 404 not found
GET /api/v1/intelligence/conclusions without bearer token -> Receive 401 unauthorized response
POST /api/v1/intelligence/flush when the queue cannot be reached -> Receive 5xx or error response indicating flush failure

features.name: Memory Profiles
features.description: Retrieve aggregated subject profiles, inspect version history, and ask natural-language questions against a subject profile.
user_flows: GET /api/v1/profiles/:subject_id with bearer token -> Receive 200 with current subject profile -> GET /api/v1/profiles/:subject_id/versions -> Receive 200 with version history -> POST /api/v1/profiles/:subject_id/ask with a natural-language question -> Receive 200 with an answer grounded in profile data
GET /api/v1/profiles/:subject_id for an unknown subject -> Receive 404 not found
POST /api/v1/profiles/:subject_id/ask with empty question text -> Receive 4xx validation error
GET /api/v1/profiles/:subject_id/versions without bearer token -> Receive 401 unauthorized response

features.name: API Key Management
features.description: Create and manage projects and API keys, grant MCP tool access, issue proxy tokens, and review usage/security analytics.
user_flows: POST /api/v1/api-keys/projects with bearer token and project details -> Receive 201 with created project -> GET /api/v1/api-keys/projects -> Receive 200 with project list -> POST /api/v1/api-keys with bearer token and project association -> Receive 201 with new API key -> GET /api/v1/api-keys -> Receive 200 with API key list -> GET /api/v1/api-keys/:keyId -> Receive 200 with key details -> PUT /api/v1/api-keys/:keyId -> Receive 200 with updated key metadata -> GET /api/v1/api-keys/analytics/usage -> Receive 200 with usage metrics -> GET /api/v1/api-keys/analytics/security-events -> Receive 200 with security events
POST /api/v1/api-keys with missing project context or invalid fields -> Receive 4xx validation error
DELETE /api/v1/api-keys/:keyId for an existing key -> Receive 200 confirming revocation/deletion
POST /api/v1/api-keys/mcp/tools with bearer token and tool grant data -> Receive 200 confirming MCP tool access grant -> GET /api/v1/api-keys/mcp/tools -> Receive 200 with grant list
POST /api/v1/api-keys/mcp/request-access without required authorization context -> Receive 401 or 403 response
POST /api/v1/api-keys/mcp/sessions/:sessionId/proxy-token with bearer token -> Receive 200 with issued proxy token

features.name: MCP API Keys & Proxy Tokens
features.description: Manage MCP session-scoped access, issue and resolve proxy tokens, inspect session status, and end sessions.
user_flows: POST /api/v1/mcp/api-keys/request-access with bearer token -> Receive 200 confirming MCP access request -> POST /api/v1/mcp/api-keys/sessions/:sessionId/keys/:keyName/proxy-token -> Receive 200 with a session-scoped proxy token -> POST /api/v1/mcp/api-keys/proxy-tokens/:proxyToken/resolve -> Receive 200 with the underlying secret or resolved key metadata -> GET /api/v1/mcp/api-keys/sessions/:sessionId/status -> Receive 200 with session status -> POST /api/v1/mcp/api-keys/sessions/:sessionId/end -> Receive 200 confirming session termination
POST /api/v1/mcp/api-keys/proxy-tokens/:proxyToken/resolve with an invalid or expired proxy token -> Receive 4xx not found or invalid token response
GET /api/v1/mcp/api-keys/sessions/:sessionId/status without bearer token -> Receive 401 unauthorized response
POST /api/v1/mcp/api-keys/sessions/:sessionId/end for an already closed session -> Receive 4xx or 200 with already-ended status

features.name: Metrics
features.description: Expose Prometheus and JSON metrics for authorized users, typically restricted to higher-tier plans.
user_flows: GET /api/v1/metrics with bearer token -> Receive 200 with Prometheus-formatted metrics text -> GET /api/v1/metrics/json with bearer token -> Receive 200 with metrics in JSON format
GET /api/v1/metrics without bearer token -> Receive 401 unauthorized response
GET /api/v1/metrics/json for a plan without access -> Receive 403 forbidden response

features.name: Service Registry
features.description: List registered services, inspect aggregated health, test auth and MCP connectivity, and trigger registry synchronization.
user_flows: GET /api/v1/services -> Receive 200 with registered service list -> GET /api/v1/services/health -> Receive 200 with aggregated downstream health -> GET /api/v1/services/auth/test -> Receive 200 with auth connectivity status -> GET /api/v1/services/mcp/test -> Receive 200 with MCP connectivity status -> POST /api/v1/services/sync with bearer token -> Receive 200 confirming registry synchronization
POST /api/v1/services/sync without bearer token -> Receive 401 unauthorized response
GET /api/v1/services/health when a downstream dependency fails -> Receive 200 with degraded health details or non-ideal status information

known_limitations.issue: Emergency admin routes mounted conditionally at /api/v1
known_limitations.impact: Privileged break-glass surface; must be disabled outside controlled environments

known_limitations.issue: Intelligence and memory-intelligence heavy ops bypass this Express server in production
known_limitations.impact: This standalone server is the self-hosted path only; production routes to Supabase Edge Functions, so behavior may differ from api.lanonasis.com

known_limitations.issue: Auth requires aligned auth middleware plus project-scope validation on all data routes
known_limitations.impact: Tests need a valid bearer token / API key and a project scope header to reach 2xx paths
